helpers.name: get_flows_for_theme
helpers.purpose: Get all flows for a theme
implementation_notes: Returns: Array of flow objects [{id, name, description, theme_id}, ...]
Queries flows WHERE theme_id = ?

used_by_directives.description: Validate theme-flow relationships before mapping - ensures consistent theme-flow associations

helpers.name: get_themes_for_flow
helpers.purpose: Get all themes for a flow
implementation_notes: Returns: Array of theme objects [{id, name, description}, ...]
Queries flow_themes junction table + themes

used_by_directives.description: Get themes for flows in dependency graph - maps flow-theme relationships

helpers.name: get_files_by_flow
helpers.purpose: Get all files for a flow
implementation_notes: Returns: Array of file objects [{id, name, path, language}, ...]
Queries file_flows junction + files table

used_by_directives.description: Query files by flow for dependency graph generation - builds flow-based dependency relationships

helpers.name: get_flows_for_file
helpers.purpose: Get all flows for a file
implementation_notes: Returns: Array of flow objects [{id, name, description, theme_id}, ...]
Queries file_flows junction + flows table

used_by_directives.description: Reverse lookup - get flows for file in dependency graph - enables bidirectional flow-file navigation

helpers.name: add_completion_path
return_statements: Consider creating milestones for this completion path: add_milestone(completion_path_id=<id>, name, status='pending', description)

During project_discovery, after creating all user-defined paths and milestones, ensure two default post-completion paths exist: 'Added Features' (status='completed', order_index=998) and 'Updates' (status='completed', order_index=999). These enable post-completion development cycles without leaving the progression system.
implementation_notes: Returns: {success: true, id: int} - new completion path ID
Inserts into completion_path table

used_by_directives.description: Create completion path stages during user request decomposition - establishes project roadmap structure

helpers.name: get_all_completion_paths
helpers.purpose: Get all completion paths ordered by order_index
implementation_notes: Returns: Array of completion path objects [{id, name, status, description, order_index}, ...]
Ordered by order_index ASC

used_by_directives.description: Query all completion paths when blueprint missing - retrieves completion path list from database for fallback
Read current completion paths for evolution tracking - compares against blueprint to detect completion path changes
Read all completion paths for status report - provides project roadmap overview

helpers.name: get_next_completion_path
helpers.purpose: Get lowest order_index with status != completed
implementation_notes: Returns: Completion path object {id, name, status, description, order_index} or null
Queries WHERE status != 'completed' ORDER BY order_index ASC LIMIT 1

used_by_directives.description: Get next incomplete completion path after milestone completes - identifies next phase of project

helpers.name: get_completion_paths_by_status
helpers.purpose: Get completion paths filtered by status
implementation_notes: Returns: Array of completion path objects [{id, name, status, description, order_index}, ...]
Queries WHERE status = ? ORDER BY order_index ASC

used_by_directives.description: Query completion paths by status for detailed status report - filters completed vs in-progress paths

helpers.name: get_incomplete_completion_paths
implementation_notes: Returns: Array of completion path objects [{id, name, status, description, order_index}, ...]
Queries WHERE status != 'completed' ORDER BY order_index ASC

used_by_directives.description: Query incomplete completion paths for status report - shows remaining work on project roadmap
Get all incomplete completion paths after milestone completes - identifies remaining project phases

helpers.name: update_completion_path
return_statements: Call add_note(note_type='evolution', directive_name='project_evolution', reference_table='completion_path', reference_id=<id>) describing the change
If status='completed': check get_incomplete_completion_paths() — if empty, route to project_completion_check directive

If reopening a completed post-completion path (status changing from 'completed' to 'in_progress' on 'Added Features' or 'Updates'): the project is no longer complete — update project status accordingly. Create milestones and tasks on-demand as work is defined for the reopened path. When the reopened path is completed again, check if all paths are complete to restore project completion.
implementation_notes: Returns: {success: true}
NULL parameters = don't update that field
Order_index cannot be updated here - use reorder helpers

used_by_directives.description: Mark completion path as completed when all milestones in path finish - updates path status

helpers.name: delete_completion_path
helpers.purpose: Delete completion path with milestone validation
implementation_notes: Returns error if milestones exist under path
Returns {success: true} after deletion with audit note
Creates note record for audit trail

helpers.name: reorder_completion_path
helpers.purpose: Change order_index for a completion path
implementation_notes: Returns: {success: true} or error if order_index conflict
Updates order_index field only
May create gaps in sequence - consider reorder_all after use

helpers.name: reorder_all_completion_paths
helpers.purpose: Fix gaps and duplicates in order_index
implementation_notes: Returns: {success: true, renumbered_count: int, duplicates_found: [{id, old_order, new_order}, ...]}
Renumbers to 1, 2, 3, 4... sequence
Preserves relative order while closing gaps

helpers.name: swap_completion_paths_order
helpers.purpose: Swap order_index of two completion paths
implementation_notes: Returns: {success: true}
Temporarily allows duplicates during swap - atomic operation
Exchanges order_index values between two paths

helpers.name: add_file_to_flow
helpers.purpose: Link a file to a flow in the file_flows junction table
helpers.error_handling: Return error if file or flow not found, or if link already exists
return_statements: File is now linked to a project flow. Verify all finalized files in the current task have flow assignments — a finalized file with 0 flows is architecturally disconnected.
implementation_notes: Returns: {success: true}
Inserts into file_flows junction table (composite PK: file_id, flow_id)
Returns error (not silent skip) if link already exists — use add_file_flows for batch with skip behavior

used_by_directives.description: Link file to flow during theme-flow mapping — assigns files to architectural flows
Link file to flow after writing — ensures all written files have flow assignments

helpers.name: add_file_flows

parameters.name: links
parameters.description: Array of [file_id, flow_id] tuples to create

helpers.purpose: Link multiple files to flows (batch) — skips existing links silently
helpers.error_handling: Return error if any file or flow ID not found
return_statements: Files are now linked to project flows. Verify all finalized files in the current task have flow assignments — a finalized file with 0 flows is architecturally disconnected.
implementation_notes: Returns: {success: true, added_count: int, skipped_count: int}
Skips existing links silently (increments skipped_count)
Validates all file_id and flow_id values exist before inserting
More efficient than multiple add_file_to_flow calls

used_by_directives.description: Batch link files to flows during theme-flow mapping — efficient for multiple file assignments

metadata.description: Flow-theme relationships, file-flow relationships, and completion path management
metadata.notes: Added add_file_to_flow and add_file_flows for file-flow junction table management
